sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
No calibrated metabolic, glucose, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, food-sensitivity result, diet recommendation, or supplement recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, environment, and professional review matter for nutrition decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated metabolic, glucose, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not make diet, supplement, food-response, nutrient, metabolic, symptom, lab, or diagnosis conclusions without validated evidence.

metabolicContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a metabolic diagnosis or treatment guide.
Bring symptoms, diet history, labs, medications, and health goals to a clinician or registered dietitian for nutrition decisions.
Do not change foods, supplements, medications, or treatment based on genotype alone.

references.title: Nutritional Genomics and Direct-to-Consumer Genetic Testing

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated metabolic, glucose, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, diet-history, symptom, lab, medication, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
Broader symptoms, food history, diet history, labs, medications, medical history, ancestry applicability, and professional assessment are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition, food response, nutrient status, symptoms, and metabolic outcomes depend on genetics plus symptoms, diet history, labs, medical history, medications, environment, and professional review.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose food reactions, nutrient deficiency, metabolic disease, or to prescribe diet or supplement changes.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not diagnose metabolic disease, diagnose diabetes, infer insulin resistance, interpret labs, prescribe a diet, recommend supplements, set nutrient targets, or provide treatment guidance.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
